text: The homelab runs on three Proxmox VE nodes connected via a 10Gb backbone switch.
Terraform with the bpg/proxmox provider manages all VM provisioning for the homelab.
Proxmox VE cluster quorum requires at least two nodes online to avoid split-brain.
Terraform state for the homelab infrastructure is stored in a local backend, not remote.
Proxmox backups run nightly to a dedicated NFS share on the NAS.
Rust is preferred for systems programming tasks requiring memory safety without garbage collection.
TypeScript is the default language for new tooling and library projects.
Python is used for one-off data analysis scripts, not production services.
Go is avoided for new projects in favor of Rust or TypeScript.
Bash scripts are kept short and delegate complex logic to TypeScript CLIs.
SQLite is chosen over Postgres for single-file, zero-infrastructure deployments.
sqlite-vec provides vector similarity search directly inside SQLite.
Postgres is still used for the OpenClaw workspace's shared multi-agent state.
WAL mode lets multiple readers access a SQLite file while one process writes.
Database backups use SQLite's online backup API rather than raw file copies.
WireGuard VPN connects remote laptops into the homelab network.
Pi-hole handles DNS filtering and ad blocking for the whole homelab.
The firewall blocks all inbound traffic except VPN and a single reverse-proxy port.
Internal services resolve via a local DNS zone rather than public DNS records.
GitHub Actions runs the CI pipeline for lint, typecheck, and tests on every push.
Docker Compose orchestrates the herd LLM host and its supporting services.
Deployments to the homelab use a git-pull-and-restart flow, no image registry.
Feature branches require a green CI run before merging to main.
Prometheus scrapes metrics from every homelab service on a 15-second interval.
Grafana dashboards visualize Proxmox node CPU, memory, and disk usage.
Log aggregation uses a lightweight Loki instance rather than the ELK stack.
Alerts for disk usage above 85 percent page through a self-hosted ntfy server.
